questions.question: Как Flink использует RocksDB Column Families?

options.text: Только default CF, всё в одном keyspace
Каждый state primitive (ValueState/MapState/ListState/etc) = отдельная CF. Своя MemTable, свои SST, свой compaction queue. Shared: WriteBufferManager, Block Cache, OptionsCache

questions.explanation: Каждый state primitive (ValueState 'count-state', MapState 'cat-counts', ListState 'history') получает свою Column Family в RocksDB. Изоляция: своя MemTable, свои SST files, свои bloom filters, своя compaction. Shared между CF через Flink shared resources: WriteBufferManager (общий MemTable budget), Block Cache (LRU cache disk blocks), OptionsCache. CF создаются lazy — при first use в open()/processElement(). Naming: <operatorId>_<stateName>.

questions.question: 10 различных counters per user, logically related. Какой подход лучше: 10 ValueState<Long> или 1 MapState<String, Long>?

options.text: 10 ValueState — каждый свою CF, лучшая isolation
1 MapState<String, Long> — меньше CF metadata overhead, better cache locality, single CF lifecycle. Lookup per counter — один get(name), ~10μs RocksDB

questions.explanation: MapState — правильный выбор для logically related counters. Trade-offs: (1) Memory overhead: 10 CF × 50KB metadata = 500KB vs 1 CF × 50KB; (2) Cache locality: blocks одного CF чаще in cache vs 10 different CFs competing; (3) Compaction: 1 CF easier to manage vs 10 separate compactions; (4) Iteration: counts.entries() для list all через range scan, vs 10 separate state.value() calls. Cost: small overhead на key serialization для composite key (kgId, ns, userKey, stateName, mapKey). На read access ~10μs RocksDB — sufficient performance. Use separate ValueState если каждый counter имеет different access pattern или TTL.
questions.studyHint: Повторите урок — separate CF vs flat key

questions.question: Custom RocksDBOptionsFactory deployed, TaskManager OOM-Killed через 6 часов. Что произошло?

options.text: Java heap size too small — увеличить

Custom factory не extends DefaultConfigurableOptionsFactory → WriteBufferManager не injected → каждая CF allocates own 64-128MB MemTable без shared budget → 10 CF × 4 slots × 128MB = 5GB native memory → OOM-Kill

questions.explanation: Самый частый production баг с RocksDB. Custom factory без WBM injection: каждая CF gets own fixed MemTable allocation (default 64MB или more), без shared budget. На state-heavy operator с 10 state primitives, 4 slots per TaskManager: 10 × 64MB × 4 = 2.5GB pure MemTable, plus Block Cache (10 × 8MB × 4 = 320MB), plus immutable MemTables awaiting flush. Total native memory exceeds managed budget → OS OOM-Killer. Fix: ALWAYS extend DefaultConfigurableOptionsFactory ИЛИ explicitly call super.createDBOptions() ИЛИ inject WriteBufferManager from Flink shared resource. Best alternative: use flink-conf properties для tuning (state.backend.rocksdb.*) instead of custom factory — preserves memory management automatically.

questions.question: Block Cache в Flink RocksDB shared между всеми Column Families в одном RocksDB instance.
questions.explanation: True. С memory.managed: true (default), Block Cache injected through Flink shared resources — единый LRU pool для всех CF. Hot blocks любого state primitive share budget. Это efficient: cache hits любой CF benefit все other CFs (нет per-CF fragmentation). Caveat: hot CF может evict blocks cold CF — нет per-CF isolation. high-prio-pool-ratio reserves часть cache для filter/index blocks (которые critical для read performance) — they pinned, не evicted легко.
